page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz, this server features 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration includes 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, achieving high read-only and write bandwidth across both small cached and large uncached blocks. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in software compilation using Clang, ray tracing, and database operations, alongside high static web serving throughput and Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall within the average range.

While the server excels in highly parallelized compute tasks and high-throughput database operations, it exhibits performance limitations in specific workloads. Large language model text generation tasks range from average to poor, with the 70B model text generation falling into the bottom 10% of benchmarks, though prompt processing speeds remain strong. This profile makes the server suitable for multi-threaded workloads, large-scale database hosting, software compilation pipelines, and high-volume web serving, whereas it is less optimal for single-threaded execution or real-time LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the 2.7 GHz ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 architecture. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory, 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to poor. The high physical core count and substantial local storage capacity offer high resource density for parallel processing. This server is suited for multi-threaded applications, high-throughput database operations, software compilation, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11,400 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for highly parallel compute and database workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal ARMv9 server with 192 dedicated cores and 11.4 TB NVMe storage, optimized for high-throughput multi-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.7 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage for high-speed data access
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operation benchmarks
Suited for software compilation, database hosting, and static web serving
Lower performance efficiency for single-threaded tasks and LLM text generation
